title: A Favor Returned After 170 Years
story: Kindness echoes through time! In 2020, an Irish group raised millions to help Native American communities struggling with COVID-19. This act of generosity was inspired by a touching story from the 1840s. Back then, the Choctaw Nation, despite facing hardships, sent money to aid Ireland during a terrible famine. On May 6, the Irish repaid this long-ago kindness, showing how compassion can span centuries.

activity_answer: US Navajo Nation and Hopi Reservation

title: Sticky Revolution: The Penny Black Arrives
story: Lick it and stick it! The world's first adhesive postage stamp made its debut in Great Britain in 1840. On May 6, the Penny Black changed mail forever. This tiny black square featured Queen Victoria's profile and cost just one penny. It made sending letters easier and more affordable for everyone, sparking a communication revolution across the country.
activity_question: What was special about the Penny Black stamp?
activity_choices: It was the world's first adhesive postage stamp
It was made of real pennies
It could only be used on Tuesdays
It played music when you licked it
activity_answer: It was the world's first adhesive postage stamp
bonus_fact: Before stamps, the person receiving the letter often had to pay for it!

title: Flying Machine Makes a Splash

title: A Simple Gift of Song
story: Joseph Brackett's melody still echoes today. Born in 1797 in Cumberland, Maine, this Shaker leader composed 'Simple Gifts,' a tune that has inspired musicians for generations. On May 6, Brackett began his journey as a religious leader and songwriter, crafting melodies that would touch hearts long after his time.

title: Trailblazing Minister Phoebe Hanaford
story: Phoebe Ann Coffin Hanaford blazed a trail for women in religion. Born in Siasconset, Massachusetts on May 6, 1829, she became the first female ordained minister in New England. Hanaford's groundbreaking achievement opened doors for women in religious leadership roles, inspiring future generations to follow their calling.
activity_question: What was Phoebe Ann Coffin Hanaford's historic achievement?
activity_choices: First female ordained minister in New England
First woman to sail around Cape Cod
First female lighthouse keeper in Siasconset
activity_answer: First female ordained minister in New England
follow_up_question: Why do you think it was important for women to become ministers?

title: A Universe-Shaping Mind Born
story: Sneek, Netherlands welcomed a brilliant baby in 1872. Willem de Sitter arrived on May 6, destined to change how we see the cosmos. This Dutch scientist grew up to become a famous cosmologist. He used Albert Einstein's ideas about gravity to create new ways of thinking about the entire universe!
activity_question: What did Willem de Sitter use to create new ways of thinking about the universe?
follow_up_question: Can you name another famous scientist who studied the universe?
